title: Los Angeles Schools Limit Screen Time

source.title: Los Angeles schools to enforce screen time limits for students

segments.title: No Screens Before Second Grade
segments.audioText: Nothing before second grade and this is part of a growing push to limit screens in classrooms amid backlash from parents and teachers.
segments.transcript: Nothing before second grade and this is part of a growing push to limit screens in classrooms amid backlash from parents and teachers.
segments.translationZh: 二年級之前完全唔用螢幕，而呢個做法係一股限制課室螢幕使用嘅趨勢之一，背後有家長同老師嘅反彈。

points.label: Nothing before second grade
points.explanationZh: Nothing 呢度係簡短講法，意思係『二年級之前完全冇螢幕時間』。

points.label: a growing push
points.explanationZh: push 係推動、倡議；growing 表示呢股趨勢愈來愈大。

points.label: amid backlash
points.explanationZh: amid 即『在……之中』；backlash 係反彈或批評。

segments.title: The District Approves a Ban
segments.audioText: So the Los Angeles Unified School District, which is the second biggest school district in the country, approved a ban on any screens in the classroom before second grade.
segments.transcript: So the Los Angeles Unified School District, which is the second biggest school district in the country, approved a ban on any screens in the classroom before second grade.
segments.translationZh: 所以全國第二大校區 Los Angeles Unified School District 批准咗一項禁令，禁止二年級之前喺課室使用任何螢幕。

points.label: which is the second biggest
points.explanationZh: which 開始補充背景；second biggest 即第二大。

points.label: approved a ban
points.explanationZh: approved 係批准；a ban 係禁令，新聞講政策時好常見。

points.label: any screens in the classroom
points.explanationZh: any screens 強調任何螢幕；in the classroom 限定係課室入面。

segments.title: Limits for Younger Students
segments.audioText: And even then, second and third graders would be limited to 20 minutes a day.
segments.transcript: And even then, second and third graders would be limited to 20 minutes a day.
segments.translationZh: 即使到咗嗰個階段，二年級同三年級學生每日都會限於 20 分鐘。

points.label: And even then
points.explanationZh: even then 表示『就算到嗰時』，用嚟強調限制仍然存在。

points.label: second and third graders
points.explanationZh: graders 指某年級學生；second and third graders 即二、三年級學生。

points.label: limited to 20 minutes a day
points.explanationZh: limited to 即限制為；a day 係每日。

segments.title: Upper Elementary Limit
segments.audioText: For fourth and fifth graders, 30 minutes a day.
segments.transcript: For fourth and fifth graders, 30 minutes a day.
segments.translationZh: 四年級同五年級學生就每日 30 分鐘。

points.label: For fourth and fifth graders
points.explanationZh: For 開頭用嚟轉去另一組人；fourth and fifth graders 即四、五年級學生。

points.label: 30 minutes
points.explanationZh: 聽數字時先抓 thirty，再聽後面 minutes 確認係時間長度。

points.label: a day
points.explanationZh: a day 放句尾，意思係每日；同 per day 一樣常見。

sentences: Nothing before second grade and this is part of a growing push to limit screens in classrooms amid backlash from parents and teachers.
So the Los Angeles Unified School District, which is the second biggest school district in the country, approved a ban on any screens in the classroom before second grade.
And even then, second and third graders would be limited to 20 minutes a day.
For fourth and fifth graders, 30 minutes a day.

vocabulary.example: Students use screens for homework and lessons.
vocabulary.translationZh: 學生會用螢幕做功課同上課。

vocabulary.example: The new rule applies inside the classroom.
vocabulary.translationZh: 新規定適用於課室入面。

vocabulary.example: The policy came after backlash from parents.
vocabulary.translationZh: 呢項政策係喺家長反彈之後出現。

vocabulary.example: The district approved new school rules.
The board approved the plan on Tuesday.
vocabulary.translationZh: 委員會星期二通過咗計劃。

vocabulary.example: The school announced a phone ban.
The rule limits screen time for younger students.
vocabulary.translationZh: 呢條規則限制低年級學生嘅螢幕時間。

phrases.phrase: a growing push to limit screens
phrases.meaningZh: 一股愈來愈大、要求限制螢幕使用嘅推動
phrases.example: There is a growing push to limit screens in schools.

phrases.phrase: amid backlash from parents and teachers
phrases.meaningZh: 在家長同老師反彈之下
phrases.example: The change came amid backlash from parents and teachers.

phrases.phrase: approved a ban on any screens
phrases.meaningZh: 批准禁止任何螢幕使用
phrases.example: The district approved a ban on any screens before second grade.
Second graders would be limited to 20 minutes a day.

speakingPractice.instructionZh: 由 00:00 跟讀 Nothing before second grade...，先分成 Nothing before second grade / and this is part of / a growing push。
重播 00:08，先聽寫 which is the second biggest school district，再補 approved a ban on any screens。
用 limited to... 練替換：limited to 10 minutes a day / limited to one hour a week / limited to school devices。
用兩句英文複述：Los Angeles schools are limiting screen time. Younger students will have the strictest limits.

notes: 今日片段係 ABC News / Good Morning America 約 1 分 20 秒嘅教育新聞，主題係洛杉磯校區限制學生螢幕時間。
聽力重點係政策新聞常見表達：approved a ban、limited to、a growing push、amid backlash。
時間碼 00:00、00:08、00:23、00:29 可以逐句重播；先盲聽數字同年級，再看稿拆 chunk。
dailyLifePractice 會延伸到學校 quick update、禮貌借用裝置同解釋家庭安排，練自然美式日常口語。

dailyLifePractice.title: School Quick Update
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你收到學校新規定，想同另一位家長簡短交換資訊。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Did you see the new screen-time policy? It starts later this year. I'm glad they're giving families some notice.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Did you see the new screen-time policy?
lines.zh: 你有冇見到新嘅螢幕時間政策？

lines.speaker: Parent
lines.en: Yeah, I just read the email.
lines.zh: 有呀，我啱啱睇咗封電郵。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I'm glad they're giving families some notice.
lines.zh: 佢哋有早啲通知家庭，我覺得幾好。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: Did you see...? 係自然開場，適合講電郵、公告、新聞。giving families some notice 即提前通知，語氣中性又禮貌。

drills.label: Substitution
drills.prompt: 將 screen-time policy 換成 lunch schedule、field trip update 或 homework policy。
drills.example: Did you see the new homework policy?

drills.label: Quick repeat
drills.prompt: 跟讀 I'm glad they're giving families some notice. 三次，語氣保持自然。
drills.example: I'm glad they're giving families some notice.

dailyLifePractice.title: Borrowing a Device Politely
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你臨時需要用電腦填表，想喺前台禮貌問可唔可以借用。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Hi, is there a computer I could use for a few minutes? I just need to fill out this form. I can be quick.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Hi, is there a computer I could use for a few minutes?
lines.zh: 你好，請問有冇電腦可以俾我用幾分鐘？

lines.speaker: Staff
lines.en: Sure, the one by the desk is available.
lines.zh: 可以，書枱旁邊嗰部可以用。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I just need to fill out this form.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: I could use... 比 I want to use... 更有禮貌。I can be quick 係美式口語，表示唔會阻人太耐。

drills.label: Polite rephrase
drills.prompt: 用 Is there a... I could use? 問可唔可以借一樣物件。
drills.example: Is there a charger I could use?

drills.label: Short response practice
drills.prompt: 對方話 Sure，你用一句英文表示感謝同會快手。

dailyLifePractice.title: Explaining a Small Delay
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你因為要處理小朋友學校通知，約會可能遲少少。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Quick heads-up: I need to handle a school email first. I might be about ten minutes late. Thanks for being flexible.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Quick heads-up: I need to handle a school email first.
lines.zh: 提提你：我需要先處理一封學校電郵。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I might be about ten minutes late.
lines.zh: 我可能會遲大約十分鐘。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: Quick heads-up 係好自然嘅『先通知你一聲』。Thanks for being flexible 比單講 sorry 更正面，適合輕微改時間。

drills.label: Substitution
drills.prompt: 將 a school email 換成 a work call、a quick errand 或 a message from the office。
drills.example: I need to handle a work call first.

drills.label: Say-it-your-way
drills.prompt: 用 Thanks for being flexible. 回應對方願意等你。
drills.example: Thanks for being flexible. I really appreciate it.
